treatment_information.treatment_response: Initially well, but at 4 months following surgery she deteriorated with a left 7th nerve palsy, right 6th nerve palsy, tetraparesis, and difficulty in swallowing.

treatment_information.specific_treatment: second shunt placement in the new cyst
treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient did not improve and developed increasing weakness and more marked bulbar disturbance.
Clinical state continued to deteriorate, and she required naso-enteric feeding and developed a marked tetraparesis.

treatment_information.specific_treatment: posterior fossa and upper cervical canal decompression with placement of a fascia lata graft (expansile duroplasty)
treatment_information.treatment_response: Immediate improvement in her weakness and 6th and 7th nerve palsies, and was soon tolerating a soft diet. Her motor function improved dramatically.

treatment_timeline.event: Second shunt placement in the new cyst
Posterior fossa and upper cervical canal decompression with placement of a fascia lata graft (expansile duroplasty)

patient_outcome.final_status: Stable neurology over many months, regained weight, continued to use a wheelchair for mobility
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow-up MRI revealed a decreasing size of the known syrinx, and gradual resolution of the oedema in the brain stem and upper cervical canal
patient_outcome.complications: Left 7th nerve palsy, right 6th nerve palsy, tetraparesis, difficulty in swallowing, marked bulbar disturbance, naso-enteric feeding requirement
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Immediate improvement post decompression surgery, stable neurology over many months
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Continued to use a wheelchair for mobility
